summary: Phase 005 subprocess reliability needs stronger lifecycle, auth, result-schema, and stress-test requirements before 24-40 sequential opencode dispatches are credible.

title: `</dev/null` fixes startup deadlock, not full 40-process lifecycle cleanup
recommendation: Require spawn stdio ignore or explicit dev-null stdin, 600s SIGTERM, grace-period SIGKILL escalation, close-event wait, and timeout metadata before continuing.

title: Provider auth preflight is missing from Phase 005's loop contract
recommendation: Run `opencode providers list` once before the loop, cache the result, fail fast or ask on missing default provider, and rerun only after auth-shaped failures.

title: Mixed success/timeout/failure records need a first-class schema before retries ship
recommendation: Define a discriminated result schema with status, attempt, condition, nullable metrics, error object, paths, sessionId, and paired-stats inclusion flag.

title: Sequential per-task JSONL avoids append races, but filename shape should include condition/attempt
recommendation: Keep sequential writes; use condition-separated paths or include condition and attempt in each JSONL row.

title: The smoke test cannot surface the 24-40 subprocess reliability risk
recommendation: Add a mocked dispatcher stress test covering at least 12 tasks x 2 conditions plus retry, timeout, metrics-missing, and final failed paths.

title: macOS fs-events watcher leakage is a subprocess cleanup concern, not a separate Phase 005 feature
recommendation: Do not modify watcher internals; make Phase 005 enforce subprocess cleanup and close-event waiting.

title: Shared DB lock risk is low for strictly sequential runs, but timeout survivors can recreate stale-lock symptoms
recommendation: Add stale-process detection, post-timeout process-tree cleanup, and a DB-readiness retry branch with a short backoff.
